objective: Improve graphify retrieval quality for fail-fast scenarios by strengthening graph-guided ranking and evaluation while keeping the slice narrowly scoped to measurable fail-fast debugging outcomes.
scope_boundary: This slice is limited to governance-owned graph retrieval and evaluation tooling, scenario corpus updates, and guidance updates tied directly to fail-fast issue retrieval quality.
This slice may update graphify measurement artifacts and guidance if the retrieval experiment produces a validated better query pattern.
This slice may create a follow-up issue if broader graph quality, report UX, or adoption work is still needed after the fail-fast retrieval target is proven or rejected.
out_of_scope: Repo-wide graph UX work such as PR comments, sweep report sections, or graph adoption dashboards.
Semantic extraction, Neo4j changes, or model fine-tuning.
research_summary: Issue #65 proved that graph-guided retrieval currently uses materially fewer retrieval tokens than bounded repo search, but it underperformed on real fail-fast questions because the measurement harness only scored graph nodes by raw label/path string overlap and ignored graph links, communities, and improved evidence scoring. The right next move is to keep #66 narrow: strengthen graph-guided ranking using the actual graph structure, improve evaluation beyond simple file-hit checks, and re-run the same fail-fast corpus before expanding into broader graph UX or adoption work.

sprints.name: Sprint 1
sprints.goal: Improve graph-guided retrieval and evaluation for fail-fast scenarios.
tasks: Add graph-aware ranking that uses links, community clustering, and file aggregation instead of only direct label/path string matching.
Improve evaluation scoring so relevance is measured by owning files plus expected evidence terms, not only exact top-file hits.
Decide whether a second non-graph baseline is needed based on measured outcomes and record that decision in the output guidance.
acceptance_criteria: At least one fail-fast scenario shows graph-guided retrieval that is both smaller and materially more relevant than the existing repo-search baseline.
The measurement output clearly explains why the graph-guided result was considered more relevant.
Governance guidance records the validated query/retrieval pattern and whether a second non-graph baseline is warranted.

specialist_reviews.focus: Keep issue #66 constrained to retrieval quality and evaluation rather than turning it into a broad graph platform rewrite.
specialist_reviews.summary: The approved direction is to focus on graph-guided ranking and evaluation against the real fail-fast corpus first. Broader graph quality, report UX, and adoption work should be follow-on issues, not folded into this acceptance path.

specialist_reviews.focus: Use the existing #65 harness as the base and strengthen it with graph-structure-aware retrieval rather than replacing the measurement flow wholesale.
specialist_reviews.summary: The current harness already gives a repeatable fail-fast corpus and token estimate path. The missing piece is graph-aware ranking and better relevance scoring, not a brand-new evaluation system.

alternate_model_review.summary: The retrieval-quality slice is narrow and can proceed with the current issue evidence plus the reviewed PDCA/R draft without a separate alternate-model review.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implement graph-aware retrieval and better fail-fast evaluation in governance only, re-run the measurement corpus, and update guidance if the new pattern is validated.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Patch the measurement harness to use graph structure and richer relevance scoring, then run the fail-fast corpus and compare the new results against the baseline.

material_deviation_rules: If the improved graph-guided retrieval still cannot beat the repo-search baseline on at least one real fail-fast scenario, do not broaden the slice silently; record that result and create or update a follow-up issue instead.
If broader graph quality or UX work becomes necessary to explain the retrieval results, issue-back that work rather than folding it into #66.
If the evaluation model needs a stronger second non-graph baseline, add it only if it remains deterministic and repo-local.
